Motion To Disqualify Pltf'S Counsel And For An Order Requiring The Return Of Sherwood'S Confidential Information
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. SCOTT A. BROWN, Esq. of Brown Poore LLP (Tel: 925-943-1166) for plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. HASSAN A. ABURISH, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips LLP (Tel: 415-940-9033) for defendant Sherwood Design Engineers, Ltd.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHERWOOD DESIGN ENGINEERS, LTD's Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel And For An Order Requiring The Return Of Sherwood's Confidential Information.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to disqualify counsel and for sanctions is granted in part and denied in part.

The motion is granted as to the USB drive. The court orders plaintiff to return any and all copies of the USB drive to defendant that plaintiff took when his employment ended with defendant. The law disfavors such "self-help" discovery. (See Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro v. Schectman (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 1279, 1289.)

The court denies disqualification of counsel. "[D]isqualification is a drastic course of action that should not be taken simply out of hypersensitivity to ethical nuances or the appearance of impropriety." (Roush v. Seagate Tech., LLC (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 210, 219.) The adduced disclosures do not require disqualification since plaintiff was entitled to disclose the information to counsel in prosecuting his wrongful termination claim. (See Neal v. Health Net, Inc. (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 831, 842-844; Fox Searchlight Pictures, Inc. v. Paladino (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 294, 314 ["[F]undamental fairness requires the plaintiff be allowed to make a limited disclosure of her former [employer's] ostensibly confidential information to her own attorneys for purposes of preparing and prosecuting a wrongful termination suit against the former [employer]."].)

The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Defendant's points and authorities seek monetary sanctions pursuant to CCP 2023.030. Defendant, however, failed to comply with CCP 2023.040 because its notice of motion does not set forth the sanction and to whom it should be assessed.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
